records.content: This section will collect key analytical questions relevant to the 2026 DRC Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak response.
This resource list is maintained by the Ebola Community of Practice. Contributions are welcome from anyone working on the 2026 DRC Ebola Bundibugyo response.

Open a GitHub Issue using our structured form:

Suggest a Resource

Fill in the fields — title, URL, type, section, authors, organisation, date, and a short description. A GitHub Action will create a draft page and open a Pull Request. A maintainer will review, adjust placement if needed, and merge.

Create a .md file in the appropriate section directory (e.g. docs/resources/dashboards/my-resource.md) and add an {include} directive to the section’s index file (e.g. docs/resources/tools-index.md). Use the format below.

Every resource page should include these mandatory metadata fields in a standardized header:# Resource Title

Type · **Organisation** · Authors · Date

Description of the resource — as much or as little as needed.

[Visit resource](https://...)

Field

Required

Example

Title

Yes

# INRB UMIE Dashboard

Type

Yes

Dashboard, Report, Tool, Dataset, Package

Organisation

Yes

Imperial College London

Authors

Yes

Ruth McCabe et al.

Date

Yes

2026-05-20

Link

Yes

[Visit resource](https://...)

Description

Yes

Free-text Markdown

Beyond the header, the page body is free-text Markdown. See the 

Formatting guide for examples of tables, code blocks, admonitions, math, and more.

We curate resources that are:

Relevant to the 2026 DRC Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak response

Publicly accessible (or with a clear access path)

Actionable — dashboards, datasets, tools, reports, and packages that responders and researchers can use

Section

What goes here

Resources and tools

Dashboards, R packages, epidemiological parameter tools

Data

Mobility, humanitarian, and epidemiological datasets

Emerging evidence

Outbreak size estimates, risk of spread, therapeutics & vaccines

Analytical questions

Key analytical questions for the outbreak response

Community meeting materials

Seminars, presentations, and meeting recordings

If a resource doesn’t fit an existing section, mention that in your issue or PR — we can create new sections as the response evolves.

A curated, community-maintained collection of resources supporting the response to the 2026 DRC Ebola Bundibugyo virus disease outbreak. This site is maintained by the Ebola Community of Practice within the 

WHO Collaboratory.

The easiest way to contribute is to 

open a GitHub Issue. Just fill in the form — no coding needed. A maintainer will review and add it to the site.

See 

Contributing for more details.

Section

What’s covered

Resources and tools

Dashboards, R packages, epidemiological parameter tools

Data

Mobility, humanitarian, and epidemiological datasets

Emerging evidence

Outbreak size estimates, risk of spread, therapeutics & vaccines

Analytical questions

Key analytical questions for the outbreak response

Community meeting materials

Seminars, presentations, and meeting recordings
This section holds links to latest situation reports:
Seminars, presentations, and recordings from community meetings.

hierarchy.lvl2: Flowminder Seminar — Mobility Data and the Outbreak
Flowminder Seminar — Mobility Data and the Outbreak

records.content:
Report · Flowminder · 2026

Recorded seminar on Flowminder mobility data in the context of the 2026 DRC Ebola outbreak.

Visit resource
Epidemiological, mobility, and humanitarian datasets.

Dataset · Flowminder · Linus Bengtsson · 2026

Monthly population-scaled inter-health-zone mobility data relevant to outbreak spread. Shorter-term mobility dynamics are in progress.

Contact 

linus​.bengtsson@flowminder​.org for access. Work is underway to release shorter-term mobility dynamics.

Visit resource

Report · Flowminder · 2026

Publicly available reports on population mobility in the DRC.

Visit resource

hierarchy.lvl2: Ebola Bundibugyo Virus Disease Crisis — HDX Event Page
Ebola Bundibugyo Virus Disease Crisis — HDX Event Page

records.content:
Dataset · OCHA / Humanitarian Data Exchange · 2026

Dedicated HDX event page bringing together DRC and Uganda datasets covering conflict events, internal displacement, population and mobility, OpenStreetMap infrastructure, and more.

Visit resource

Report · Imperial College London (MRC-IDE) · 2026

Dedicated page for Bundibugyo virus epidemiological parameters within the epireview documentation.

Visit resource
Recent analyses and assessments related to the 2026 DRC Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak.

hierarchy.lvl2: Estimation of the size of the outbreak of Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus in the DRC
Estimation of the size of the outbreak of Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus in the DRC

records.content:
Report · Imperial College London · Ruth McCabe et al. · 2026-05-20

May 20, 2026 update. Estimates of the current outbreak size using epidemiological modelling approaches.

Visit resource

hierarchy.lvl2: Estimating the current size of the 2026 DRC Bundibugyo virus outbreak
Estimating the current size of the 2026 DRC Bundibugyo virus outbreak

records.content:
Report · LSHTM · Sam Abbott, Samuel Brand, Sebastian Funk · 2026

A joint Bayesian re-analysis of the McCabe et al. report, providing independent size estimates for the 2026 DRC Bundibugyo outbreak.

Visit resource

hierarchy.lvl2: Ebola BVD — DRC Spread Risk Assessment #1
Ebola BVD — DRC Spread Risk Assessment #1

records.content:
Report · Epistorm · Alex Vespignani · 2026

Spread risk assessment for Ebola Bundibugyo virus disease in the DRC, shared via the Epistorm network.

hierarchy.lvl2: Risk of international importation of Bundibugyo Ebola Virus Disease to Europe
Risk of international importation of Bundibugyo Ebola Virus Disease to Europe

records.content:
Report · Sorbonne Universite, INSERM, SUMOC · Federico Fanelli, Francesco Parino, Chiara Poletto, Vittoria Colizza · 2026

Assessment of the risk of international importation to Europe following the 2026 PHEIC declaration. Report #2 on Ebola risk of international spread.

WHO position on use of Ervebo for Bundibugyo Virus Disease

Official WHO position statement on the use of the Ervebo vaccine for Bundibugyo Virus Disease.

WHO guidance on therapeutics prioritisation for Bundibugyo Virus Disease.

Tools, dashboards, and packages supporting the Ebola outbreak response.

hierarchy.lvl2: INRB UMIE Dashboard — DRC Ebola Bundibugyo 2026
INRB UMIE Dashboard — DRC Ebola Bundibugyo 2026

records.content:
Dashboard · INRB · INRB, UMIE · 2026

Real-time epidemiological dashboard tracking the DRC Ebola Bundibugyo 2026 outbreak, maintained by INRB and UMIE.

Visit resource

hierarchy.lvl2: Immunization for Resilience — Ebola BVD Outbreak 2026
Immunization for Resilience — Ebola BVD Outbreak 2026

records.content:
Dashboard · Immunization for Resilience · 2026

Interactive Shiny dashboard to explore outbreak trajectory, geographic spread, response pillars, and situation updates.

Visit resource

Package · LSHTM / Epiverse-TRACE · 2026

R package for working with epidemiological parameters, developed by LSHTM through the Epiverse-TRACE initiative.

Visit resource

Tool · LSHTM / Epiverse-TRACE · 2026

Vignette demonstrating how to use the epireview package together with epiparameter for Ebola epidemiological analysis.

Visit resource

Package · Imperial College London (MRC-IDE) · 2026

Interface to explore the latest data, figures, and tables curated by PERG for Ebola epidemiological parameters.

Visit resource

Tool · Imperial College London · PERG · 2026

A set of 330 Ebola epidemiological parameters, of which 23 are Bundibugyo virus, extracted through systematic reviews by PERG. Available along with their original source publications.

Visit resource
